user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows the claimed object (car) and the claimed area (rear bumper) with visible damage.
claim_status_justification: Image 'img_1' clearly shows extensive denting and deformation across the entire rear bumper, consistent with the user's claim of a new dent on the rear of their car. The damage is severe and also affects the trunk lid area.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted images show two distinct vehicles, preventing a consistent assessment of the claimed damage.
claim_status_justification: Image 'img_1' shows a white Jaguar without any visible scratch on its front bumper. Image 'img_2' depicts severe damage (dent and broken part) on the front bumper of a different white car, which does not match the vehicle in 'img_1'. Consequently, the specific claim of a scratch on the front bumper cannot be supported or contradicted for a single, identified vehicle.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Images provide contradictory evidence regarding the presence of damage to the windshield.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 clearly shows a cracked windshield, consistent with the claim. However, image img_2, appearing to be of the same car's windshield, shows it entirely intact with no visible damage. This direct contradiction in the visual evidence means the claim cannot be definitively supported or contradicted by the provided images.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the claimed side mirror and its damage.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 clearly shows the side mirror with extensive shattering of the mirror glass and visible damage to the mirror casing.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image img_1 clearly shows multiple dents and scratches on the rear bumper of the claimed vehicle, providing sufficient visual evidence to assess the claim.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 clearly shows multiple dents and associated scratches on the silver rear bumper, consistent with the customer's claim of 'bumper damage' after being 'tapped from behind'. The damage is distinctly visible on the surface.
